items.title: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington dinner
items.summary: Investigators say the 31-year-old California man wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible.

items.title: US political violence generates a familiar cycle - this time it's in overdrive
items.summary: In modern America, it seems violence of this kind has become an ever-present storm that can strike anywhere and at any moment.

items.title: Jimmy Kimmel rejects White House criticism over Melania widow joke
items.summary: In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".

items.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
items.summary: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

items.title: Austrian goes on trial accused of plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert
items.summary: Prosecutors say the man, 21-year-old Beran A, received training from members of jihadist group Islamic State on how to handle explosives.

items.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
items.summary: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

items.title: IS says it was behind deadly Nigeria attack on football pitch
items.summary: Authorities say the militants raided a football pitch where people had been gathering and opened fire at random.

items.title: Rescuers race to free survivors trapped inside train after fatal Indonesia crash
items.summary: Two trains collided on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital on Monday, killing at least 15 people.

items.title: Venice opera house drops incoming music director after nepotism remarks
items.summary: Beatrice Venezi's appointment had been marred in controversy since being announced last September, prompting orchestra members to go on strike.

items.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
items.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

items.title: Russian fighters confirm withdrawal from northern Mali city after separatist attacks
items.summary: Ethnic Tuareg fighters say they are in control of Kidal following a weekend of nationwide attacks, alongside Islamist groups.

items.title: Gunmen raid Nigerian orphanage and kidnap children
items.summary: The attack saw 23 children and the facility's proprietress kidnapped by gunmen on Sunday.

items.title: Michael Jackson biopic smashes box office record
items.summary: The film received poor reviews but has stormed the box office, taking $217m (£160m) globally.

items.title: At least 42 killed in Chad after water well dispute escalates
items.summary: The dispute was initially between two families before escalating into a cycle of reprisal attacks.

items.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
items.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

items.title: French coastguard rescues more than 100 migrants crossing Channel
items.summary: A boat carrying 106 people broke down at sea, while others were rescued attempting to board the vessel.

items.title: Plane crash in South Sudan kills all 14 on board
items.summary: The authorities are investigating but early reports suggest bad weather and poor visibility may be to blame.

items.title: Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
items.summary: In a rare interview, Commander Robert Brovdi shared how his unit accounts for a third of all targets destroyed on the battlefield.

items.title: How Kenya's Sabastian Sawe broke iconic sub-two-hour barrier at the London Marathon
items.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe on becoming the first person to break the two-hour barrier for the marathon.

items.title: Canada's Carney launches a sovereign wealth fund. What is it?
items.summary: The fund - which Canadians can invest in directly - will help pay for major infrastructure projects in the country, the prime minister says.

items.title: What we're learning about suspected Washington press dinner gunman
items.summary: The man arrested at the event attended by President Trump appeared in court on Monday.

items.title: The Chinese sports brand taking on Nike and Adidas
items.summary: Now one of the biggest sportswear firms, Anta's rise follows a playbook adopted by many Chinese giants.

items.title: Remembering Raghu Rai: The photographer who showed India to itself
items.summary: With Rai's passing, India has lost a visual historian who was a chronicler of its most defining and often painful moments.

items.title: Performer describes locking eyes with Trump as they ducked for cover during shooting
items.summary: Oz Pearlman - a self-described "mentalist" - was speaking to Trump, First Lady Melania Trump and press secretary Karoline Leavitt as the attack happened.

items.title: What happened when Rebel Wilson gave evidence in court?
items.summary: The Hollywood star is accused by fellow Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes of making defamatory statements.

items.title: Inside Trump press dinner shooting suspect's court appearance
items.summary: BBC reports from Washington DC, where Cole Tomas Allen was charged with attempting to assassinate Donald Trump.

items.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
items.summary: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

items.title: Video shows correspondents' dinner suspect charge checkpoint
items.summary: Surveillance video released by Donald Trump shows the suspect running past metal detectors as security agents draw their guns.

items.title: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
items.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.
